steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice 'emphasizes a difference between the two birds.' A valid answer must mention both birds and frame the information as a contrast, not merely a shared trait or a description of only one.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Though they share... is larger': uses a concessive structure to acknowledge similarity while highlighting a size difference between the two birds. Both birds appear and a contrast is drawn. ✓
  • Choice B: describes only one of the two birds; the other is not mentioned. ✗
  • Choice C: mentions both birds but focuses on what they share, not a difference. ✗
  • Choice D: describes only one of the two birds. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
